description: Sitework phase closes when the lot is cleared, graded, and stabilized; underground utilities are stubbed and located; erosion controls are active and inspected; and the building pad is compacted, surveyed, and approved for foundation work to begin.

items.label: Building permit card posted on-site in weatherproof holder, visible from street
Septic / sewer permit approved and copy on-site (Baldwin County Health Department)
Land-disturbing activity permit confirmed if disturbed area exceeds 1 acre (ADEM NPDES requirement)
Driveway / access permit issued by Baldwin County Engineering if county road access is required
Wetlands determination letter on file; Army Corps 404 permit confirmed if any fill within jurisdictional wetlands
Tree-save fence installed at drip line of all trees designated for preservation on site plan
Clearing limits staked and flagged to match approved site plan — no clearing beyond staked boundary
All trees, stumps, and organic debris removed from building footprint and within 10 feet of foundation perimeter
Demolished concrete, asphalt, or existing structure materials hauled off-site; no burial of organics or demolition debris on lot
Topsoil stripped from building footprint and stockpiled on-site per grading plan for reuse or disposal
Silt fence installed along downslope perimeter of all disturbed areas before any grading begins
Construction entrance stabilized with 6-inch minimum compacted #57 stone pad, minimum 50 feet long, to prevent mud tracking onto road
Inlet protection installed on any storm drain inlets within 100 feet of disturbed area
Sediment basin or sediment trap installed if disturbed area exceeds threshold per ADEM SWPPP requirements
SWPPP binder on-site, signed by qualified person, with inspection log started — first inspection documented before grading commences
All erosion controls inspected by superintendent after first rain event and after any storm exceeding 0.5 inch; deficiencies corrected within 24 hours
Rough grade matches approved site plan finish floor elevation (FFE) within +/- 0.10 foot; FFE confirmed against flood zone BFE plus required freeboard
Positive drainage slope established away from building pad — minimum 6 inches of fall in first 10 feet on all sides per IRC R401.3
Swales cut to plan dimensions and flow direction confirmed by level; no ponding low spots within 20 feet of foundation
Fill material is clean select fill, free of organics, debris, and unsuitable soils; source and material type documented
Cut and fill quantities reconciled against grading plan; any deviation from plan approved in writing by engineer of record before proceeding
Sewer lateral or septic system stub-out installed to within 5 feet of building footprint at correct invert elevation per engineered plan
Water service tap and meter set by Baldwin County Water Authority (or applicable utility); service line sleeved under any driveways or hardscape
Electrical service conduit stubbed to within 5 feet of meter location; conduit depth complies with NEC 300.5 burial depth requirements
Gas service line (if applicable) installed and pressure-tested per utility provider requirements before backfill
All underground utility locations marked with color-coded tracer wire or conduit; as-built sketch with depths documented and filed
All trench backfill compacted in maximum 8-inch lifts to 95% Standard Proctor per geotechnical report requirements; no water jetting used as compaction method
Geotechnical engineer or certified testing lab has proofrolled subgrade with loaded dump truck; no pumping, rutting, or soft spots observed
Compaction testing (nuclear gauge or sand cone) confirms minimum 95% Standard Proctor density at subgrade and at each 12-inch lift of fill — test reports signed and on file
Pad elevation surveyed by licensed surveyor and confirmed to match engineered finish floor elevation within +/- 0.05 foot
All tree stumps, roots larger than 1.5 inches diameter, and organic material removed from footprint to minimum 24 inches below finish subgrade
Vapor retarder or capillary break layer (if required by foundation engineer) placed and intact across full footprint before any form work begins
Superintendent and foundation sub have walked pad together and signed off that subgrade is acceptable to proceed to foundation
Baldwin County Building Department setback / survey inspection passed (if required prior to foundation); inspection card signed
Compaction test reports reviewed and approved by project engineer of record in writing
SWPPP inspection log current with no unresolved deficiencies noted
Septic system installation inspected and approved by Baldwin County Health Department before any backfill over tank or field lines
Superintendent has completed site walk against this checklist; all open items resolved or written into a dated punch-log with assigned responsible party and due date
Client notified in writing that Sitework phase is closed and foundation phase is authorized to begin
